rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH DICK VS. LA GALLERIA CONDOMINIUM OWNERS' ASSOCIATION, A NON
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Bifrcate Case And Try The Equitable Claims And Defenses In Plaintiff?S Complaint In Priority
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion calendar for Monday, December 23, 2024, Line 1. RESPONDENT LA GALLERIA CONDOMINIUM OWNERS ASSOCIATION's Motion To Bifurcate Case And Try The Equitable Claims And Defenses In Plaintiff's Complaint In Priority. The motion to bifurcate is transferred to Department 206 on the day of trial, April 7, 2025, to be heard by the assigned trial judge. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/VH)

rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion calendar for Monday, December 23, 2024, Line 4. MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD AS TO DEFENDANT GT HAULING LLC. The unopposed motion for withdrawal of attorney of record is granted. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302VHM)

rulings.Case Title: KUDRAT DUTTA CHAUDHARY VS. LAW OFFICES OF ROBERT B. JOBE, P.C. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs Request For Production Of Documents, Set One
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Discovery Calendar for Monday, December 23, 2024, Line 5. 2 - PLAINTIFF KUDRAT CHAUDHARY's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs Request For Production Of Documents, Set One. Plaintiff's motion to compel further responses to plaintiff's request for production (set one) as to defendant Robert Jobe is denied as moot. The record reflects that since plaintiff filed this motion, supplemental responses have been provided. (Whang Decl., Exs. 1 and 2; Sinaiko Healthcare Consulting, Inc. v. Pac. Healthcare Consultants (2007) 148 Cal.App. 4th 390, 408-09 [finding that a trial court has discretion in deciding whether to rule on a motion to compel after a party serves a response].) The parties are ordered to meet and confer regarding the propriety of the supplemental responses. If necessary, plaintiff may file a new motion to compel further responses. Plaintiff is awarded $5,000 from defendant because there was no substantial justification for his initial inadequate response and delay. (Cal. Rules of Court, rule 3.1348(a) ["The court may award sanctions under the Discovery Act in favor of a party who files a motion to compel discovery, even though ... the requested discovery was provided to the moving party after the motion was filed"].) For the 9:00 a.m. Discovery calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/VMH)
